---
title: "EDA<br>Stock Prices & Volume VN30 Index Vietnam"

author: "**Pham_Dinh_Bao_Khoi** (Étudiant)<br>khoipdb.23bi14230@usth.edu.vn"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  rmdformats::readthedown:
    highlight: espresso
    code_download: TRUE
    code_folding: show
    number_sections: TRUE
    dev: "svg"
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: sentence
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, fig.dim = c(8, 6), fig.align = "center", out.width = "100%")
```

```{r klippy, echo=FALSE, include=TRUE}
klippy::klippy(c('r', 'python', 'json', 'linux'), position = c('top', 'right'),
               tooltip_message = 'Click to copy', tooltip_success = 'Copied')
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------
# file management
```{r}
#install.packages("tidyverse")
#install.packages("lubridate") # For Date handling
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(fs) # Helps with file paths

# 1. Set the path to your dataset folder
# Replace with the actual path where you unzipped the Kaggle data
data_path <- "/home/palm/ASTD/stock_price_VN30" 

# 2. Get a list of all CSV file paths
file_paths <- list.files(path = data_path, pattern = "*.csv", full.names = TRUE)

# Check if files were found
print(paste("Found", length(file_paths), "files."))

# 3. Function to read a file and add the Ticker column
read_stock_file <- function(file) {
  # Extract the filename (e.g., "ACB.csv") and remove extension to get "ACB"
  ticker_name <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file))
  
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE) %>% 
    mutate(Ticker = ticker_name) %>%   # Add Ticker column
    clean_names()                      # Optional: requires 'janitor' pkg, standardizes column names
}

# 4. Read and Combine all files into one Data Frame
# map_dfr loops through 'file_paths', runs 'read_stock_file', and binds rows together
vn30_data <- map_dfr(file_paths, read_stock_file)

# Preview the combined data
glimpse(vn30_data)

```


```{r}
# Convert Date column to Date type (Check your data format first!)

vn30_clean <- vn30_data %>%
  mutate(trading_date = dmy(trading_date)) %>%  
  arrange(ticker, trading_date)

# Check for missing values
colSums(is.na(vn30_clean))

# Remove duplicates if any
vn30_clean <- vn30_clean %>% distinct()
```
